top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The US-Iran truce is fraying fast: after a drone attack on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz, US forces struck Iranian missile and radar sites and Iran says it hit back at American positions, with Bahrain reporting drone strikes. Rescuers in Venezuela are digging through rubble after twin earthquakes killed more than 920 people, while a record-breaking heatwave grips Europe. There was a rare diplomatic bright spot — Israel and Lebanon signed a US-brokered framework deal — alongside a busy day of US politics and a deadly plane crash into Beijing's tallest tower.

stories.headline: US strikes Iran after Hormuz cargo-ship attack; Tehran says it hit back
stories.summary: US Central Command said it struck Iranian missile and drone storage sites and coastal radar after a drone attack on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz, which Washington called a ceasefire violation. Iran's Revolutionary Guard said it struck US-linked positions in response; Bahrain reported being hit by 'several Iranian drones' and UKMTO said a tanker's bridge was damaged by a projectile, with all crew safe. It is the worst escalation since the 17 June truce.
stories.source: BBC, Channel NewsAsia, The Straits Times

stories.headline: Venezuela earthquake death toll tops 920 as rescuers search for missing
stories.summary: The death toll from twin earthquakes in Venezuela has risen above 920 with thousands still missing, as international rescue teams arrive and residents dig through rubble by hand. Hospitals in the worst-hit coastal areas near La Guaira are overwhelmed, and the disaster has compounded an already fragile political and economic situation.

stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon sign US-brokered framework peace agreement
stories.summary: Israel and Lebanon signed a 14-point framework agreement in Washington after days of US-mediated talks, affirming each state's right to live in peace and tasking the Lebanese Armed Forces with restoring sovereignty pending the disarmament of non-state armed groups. Hezbollah, which is not a party to the deal, has not agreed to withdraw; Israeli PM Netanyahu said troops will stay in southern Lebanon until Hezbollah disarms.

stories.headline: Record heatwave breaks German temperature record as it pushes east
stories.summary: A deadly heatwave broke Germany's temperature record and forced the cancellation of public events as it moved east toward Poland. Authorities across Europe issued high-alert warnings, with experts warning of drastic glacier loss in the Alps and drought drying Italy's longest river.
stories.source: BBC, Channel NewsAsia, The Straits Times

stories.headline: Ex-Trump adviser John Bolton pleads guilty to mishandling classified documents
stories.summary: John Bolton, a former national security adviser turned Trump critic, pleaded guilty to mishandling classified documents. The plea closes one of several high-profile legal cases involving former senior US officials.
stories.source: BBC, Channel NewsAsia, The Straits Times

stories.headline: Seoul says Chinese and Russian military aircraft entered its air defence zone
stories.summary: South Korea said Chinese and Russian military aircraft entered its air defence identification zone, prompting it to scramble fighter jets. Such joint incursions have recurred in recent years and add to regional tensions around the Korean peninsula.

stories.headline: Small plane crashes into Beijing's tallest building, pilot killed
stories.summary: A small aircraft crashed into the CITIC Tower, Beijing's tallest building, killing the pilot, authorities said. Eyewitnesses reported the plane striking the skyscraper; the cause is under investigation.

stories.headline: Trump unveils new US passport featuring his own picture
stories.summary: President Donald Trump unveiled a redesigned US passport bearing a picture of himself, drawing both attention and criticism. The redesign is the latest in a series of personalised changes to federal symbols under his administration.

stories.headline: Texas school board approves mandated reading list including Bible passages
stories.summary: The Texas state school board approved a mandated reading list that includes Bible stories and passages, reigniting debate over religion in public education. Supporters frame it as teaching foundational literature; critics call it a breach of church-state separation.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: Expect thundery showers tonight and tomorrow afternoon, with temperatures up to 34°C. On the roads, LTA and police wrapped up a nine-day blitz that seized more than 100 illegal active-mobility devices and vehicles and flagged 380 illegal modifications. Other talking points: a candid MOE session on easing Singapore's fixation with grades, a Singaporean man surviving a fatal crash near KL, and Tiong Bahru becoming the first estate to scrap its blue recycling bins.

stories.headline: LTA-police blitz seizes 100+ illegal mobility devices, flags 380 illegal vehicle mods
stories.summary: A nine-day joint operation by the Land Transport Authority and police seized more than 100 active mobility devices and motor vehicles and uncovered some 380 illegal vehicle modifications islandwide, including a BMW with a laser-engraved number plate. Authorities said the crackdown targets road-safety risks from non-compliant devices and vehicles.
stories.source: Channel NewsAsia, The Straits Times, Mothership

stories.headline: How to move past the fixation on grades? Big questions at MOE engagement session
stories.summary: Parents, educators and students raised pointed questions about easing Singapore's fixation on academic grades at the Education Ministry's first engagement session, part of a broader review of how success is defined in schools. The discussion touched on stress, holistic development and how to broaden definitions of achievement.

stories.headline: Singaporean man survives car crash near KL; two fellow passengers killed
stories.summary: A Singaporean man escaped with minor injuries in a car crash near Kuala Lumpur, but two fellow passengers were killed, according to The Straits Times. The circumstances of the crash are under investigation by Malaysian authorities.

stories.headline: Tiong Bahru becomes first estate to scrap blue recycling bins permanently
stories.summary: Tiong Bahru residents are doing away with the communal blue recycling bins permanently, in a shift toward alternative recycling arrangements for the estate. The move is being watched as a test case for how Singapore handles household recycling.

stories.headline: Two brothers diagnosed with Sanfilippo syndrome, a rare 'childhood dementia'
stories.summary: The Straits Times profiled two young brothers diagnosed with Sanfilippo syndrome, a rare and brutal genetic disorder dubbed 'childhood dementia' that progressively robs children of their abilities. The family's account highlights the challenges of caring for children with ultra-rare conditions in Singapore.

stories.headline: Migrant workers left anxious by unpaid wages weigh job hunts or returning home
stories.summary: Some migrant workers caught in an unpaid-wages dispute linked to KPA Engineering are anxiously seeking new jobs while others consider going home, Channel NewsAsia reported. The case has renewed scrutiny of protections for foreign workers when employers run into financial trouble.

stories.headline: Two Singaporean girls, 13 and 14, recount why they ran away from home
stories.summary: In a Mothership feature, two teenage girls shared what led them to run away from home and how they felt afterward, offering a candid look at youth and family pressures. The piece struck a chord locally as a window into struggles many young Singaporeans face quietly.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: The US-Iran truce is under its heaviest strain yet: after a drone attack on a Hormuz cargo ship, US forces struck Iranian missile and radar sites and Iran says it retaliated against American positions, with Bahrain reporting drone strikes. In a contrasting move, Israel and Lebanon signed a US-brokered framework deal, though Hezbollah is not a party and Israeli troops remain in southern Lebanon. In Ukraine, Kyiv said it struck a defence plant in Russia's Volgograd region.

theatres.name: US–Iran / Strait of Hormuz
theatres.status: Truce strained — tit-for-tat strikes; worst escalation since the 17 June deal

timeline.event: US and Israeli attacks against Iran begin; Iran effectively closes the Strait of Hormuz, spiking global oil prices and choking shipments of oil, gas and commodities such as fertiliser.
US and Iran agree a 14-point memorandum of understanding to end hostilities; Iran commits to use its 'best efforts' for safe passage of commercial vessels, free of charge, for 60 days.
The UN nuclear chief says IAEA inspectors will visit Iranian sites as part of the war deal.
A drone attack on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz halts a planned evacuation of stranded sailors; Tehran says the ship was hit because it used an unauthorised route through the waterway.

US Central Command strikes Iranian missile and drone storage facilities and coastal radar positions, calling it 'a powerful response'. Iran's IRGC says its navy struck US military positions; Bahrain reports being hit by 'several Iranian drones'; UKMTO says a tanker's bridge was damaged by a projectile, with all crew safe.
timeline.source: BBC, Channel NewsAsia, The Straits Times

theatres.name: Israel–Lebanon (Hezbollah)
theatres.status: Framework deal signed; Israeli troops remain, Hezbollah not a party

timeline.event: Limited fighting continues between Israeli forces and Iranian-backed Hezbollah in southern Lebanon despite an existing ceasefire, with both sides trading cross-border fire and accusing the other of violations; Israel occupies around 5% of Lebanese territory.

Israel and Lebanon sign a 14-point framework agreement in Washington after US-mediated talks. The Lebanese Armed Forces are to restore sovereign authority pending the disarmament of non-state armed groups; President Aoun calls it a first step, while PM Netanyahu says Israeli forces will stay until Hezbollah disarms. Hezbollah is not a party to the deal.
A senior Ukrainian intelligence official is jailed for life for spying for Russia, BBC reports, underscoring continued counter-intelligence pressure inside Ukraine.
Ukraine struck a defence plant in Russia's Volgograd region, President Zelensky said, as long-range strikes on military-industrial targets continue on both sides.

outbreaks.label: Outbreaks
outbreaks.ai_summary: The Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak in DR Congo is the dominant health emergency: national authorities reported about 1,115 confirmed cases and 304 deaths in DRC as of 26 June, prompting the US CDC to activate its highest-level (Level 1) response and ship an experimental treatment. Uganda, with 20 cases and 2 deaths, says containment is working. Elsewhere, India confirmed a single Nipah virus case in Kerala, and the WHO flagged rising yellow fever risk across Africa and the Americas.

outbreaks.name: Ebola (Bundibugyo virus) — DR Congo & Uganda
outbreaks.status: Accelerating in DRC; contained in Uganda; US CDC at Level 1 response
outbreaks.scope_note: Confirmed cases, DRC + Uganda combined: DRC ~1,115 cases/304 deaths and Uganda 20 cases/2 deaths per national authorities and US CDC (via AFP), 26 Jun. WHO's last formal DON (DON608, 19 Jun) confirmed 915 cases and 234 deaths; figures are rising and agency tallies lag. No approved vaccine or treatment exists for the Bundibugyo strain.

outbreaks.name: Nipah virus — India (Kerala)
outbreaks.status: Single confirmed case; contact tracing under way
outbreaks.scope_note: One laboratory-confirmed Nipah case (adult male, Kozhikode district, Kerala), confirmed by RT-PCR and reported to WHO; patient is on ventilator support, no deaths reported. Extensive contact tracing initiated (WHO DON609, 25 Jun).

outbreaks.name: Yellow fever — Global (Africa & Americas)
outbreaks.status: Elevated transmission risk; WHO global alert
outbreaks.scope_note: WHO global risk assessment (DON610, 24 Jun), not a single point outbreak: sustained sylvatic transmission in Africa and a notable rise in the Americas, including spread into lower-risk zones. Figures are WHO's annual global estimates (67,000–173,000 severe cases; 31,000–82,000 deaths), not a single-outbreak count; vaccine coverage in many high-risk countries remains below target.

stories.headline: US activates highest-level Ebola response, ships experimental treatment to DRC and Uganda
stories.summary: US health authorities activated a Level 1 response — the highest, last used for the 2014-2016 epidemic — to the Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in DR Congo, while stressing the risk to the US remains low. The CDC said it is sending doses of an experimental treatment (MBP134) to DRC and Uganda and offering thousands of tests; the epidemic has claimed about 304 lives out of roughly 1,115 confirmed cases in DRC, with 20 cases and 2 deaths in Uganda.
stories.source: The Straits Times, AFP, US CDC

stories.headline: WHO: Bundibugyo Ebola cases pass 900 as DRC outbreak spreads rapidly
stories.summary: In its latest Disease Outbreak News (19 June), the WHO reported a rapid rise to 915 confirmed cases (896 in DRC, 19 in Uganda) and 234 deaths, with at least 88 recoveries. The agency warned transmission is amplified in health-care settings with inadequate infection control and during unsafe burials; national tallies have since climbed further.

stories.headline: India confirms Nipah virus case in Kerala; contact tracing under way
stories.summary: The WHO said India confirmed a Nipah virus infection in Kozhikode district, Kerala — an adult male who developed neurological symptoms and is on ventilator support in intensive care. Extensive contact tracing began after the case was confirmed by RT-PCR at the National Institute of Virology in Pune. Nipah is a zoonotic virus spread by fruit bats and contaminated fruit, with limited person-to-person transmission.

stories.headline: WHO flags rising yellow fever risk across Africa and the Americas
stories.summary: A WHO global update warned of sustained yellow fever transmission in Africa and a notable rise in the Americas, including spread into lower-risk zones, driven by rainfall, temperature and mosquito ecology. Twenty-seven African and 13 Central/South American countries are considered high-risk, and vaccination coverage in many remains below target, raising the risk of urban outbreaks and international spread.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Actively exploited flaws lead the cycle: CISA added a critical PTC Windchill RCE (CVE-2026-12569, CVSS 9.8) to its KEV catalogue amid web-shell attacks, and set an urgent deadline for a Cisco Unified CM flaw (CVE-2026-20230). Nation-state activity is busy — the FBI warns Russian intelligence hackers are targeting Signal backup recovery keys, and Google detailed a new Turla backdoor in Ukraine. Watch the supply chain too: Polymarket users lost $3M, a 10M-install Chrome ad blocker hid injection code, and Miasma malware hit npm and GitHub Actions.

stories.headline: FBI warns Russian intelligence hackers are targeting Signal backup recovery keys
stories.summary: The FBI warned that Russian intelligence-linked hackers are targeting Signal's backup recovery keys to gain access to encrypted messages, urging users to protect their recovery credentials. The campaign reflects continued state-sponsored interest in compromising secure-messaging accounts of high-value targets.

stories.headline: CISA sets urgent deadline to patch exploited Cisco Unified CM flaw
stories.summary: CISA set an urgent remediation deadline after a Cisco Unified Communications Manager server-side request forgery flaw (CVE-2026-20230) was added to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalogue and seen exploited in attacks. Administrators are urged to apply Cisco's fixes immediately.

stories.headline: New SharkLoader malware deploys Cobalt Strike in 'StrikeShark' attacks
stories.summary: Researchers detailed a new loader, SharkLoader, used to deploy Cobalt Strike beacons in a campaign dubbed StrikeShark. The loader uses evasion techniques to stage the post-exploitation framework on compromised hosts.

stories.headline: Polymarket customers lose $3 million in supply-chain attack
stories.summary: Customers of prediction-market platform Polymarket lost about $3 million after a supply-chain attack compromised a component in its delivery chain, redirecting funds. The incident underscores the growing risk of third-party and dependency compromises in fintech and crypto platforms.

stories.headline: Cybersecurity firms targeted by fraudulent OpenAI organisation invites
stories.summary: Attackers are sending fraudulent OpenAI 'organisation invite' emails to cybersecurity firms in a social-engineering campaign aimed at harvesting credentials or delivering malware. The lure abuses trust in widely used AI platform notifications.

stories.headline: Chinese-speaking APT deploys new TinyRCT backdoor in Southeast Asia campaign
stories.summary: A Chinese-speaking advanced persistent threat group is deploying a new backdoor, TinyRCT, in a campaign targeting Southeast Asia. The tooling gives operators persistent remote access to compromised networks in the region.

stories.headline: New Linux 'pedit' copy-on-write exploit enables root via poisoned cached binaries
stories.summary: Researchers disclosed a new copy-on-write exploitation technique, dubbed pedit, that lets a local attacker gain root on Linux by poisoning cached binaries. The flaw class echoes earlier Dirty COW-style memory-handling weaknesses and warrants prompt patching once fixes land.

stories.headline: Amazon Q Developer flaw could let malicious repos run code via MCP configs
stories.summary: A flaw in Amazon Q Developer could allow a malicious repository to execute code on a developer's machine by abusing Model Context Protocol (MCP) configuration handling. Opening or working with a booby-trapped repo could trigger code execution, highlighting risks in AI coding-assistant integrations.

stories.headline: CISA adds actively exploited PTC Windchill RCE to KEV as web-shell attacks continue
stories.summary: CISA added a critical PTC Windchill and FlexPLM improper-input-validation flaw (CVE-2026-12569, CVSS 9.8) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalogue as attackers continue to deploy web shells via the bug. The remote-code-execution flaw affects product-lifecycle-management deployments; organisations should patch immediately.

stories.headline: New 'DirtyClone' Linux kernel flaw lets local users gain root via cloned packets
stories.summary: A newly disclosed Linux kernel flaw, DirtyClone, allows a local user to escalate to root by abusing cloned network packets. Local privilege-escalation bugs like this are valuable to attackers chaining footholds into full system compromise.

stories.headline: Miasma malware targets npm packages and GitHub Actions in supply-chain attack
stories.summary: A malware campaign dubbed Miasma is targeting npm packages and GitHub Actions workflows to compromise software supply chains, allowing attackers to inject malicious code into downstream builds. Developers are urged to audit dependencies and pin/verify CI/CD actions.

stories.headline: Microsoft warns of photo ZIP phishing campaign hitting hotels with Node.js implant
stories.summary: Microsoft warned of a phishing campaign targeting the hospitality sector that uses ZIP archives posing as photos to deliver a Node.js-based implant to hotel systems. The lure exploits booking and guest-photo workflows to gain a foothold.

stories.headline: Google details Turla's new STOCKSTAY backdoor used in Ukraine espionage
stories.summary: Google detailed a new backdoor, STOCKSTAY, used by the Russia-linked Turla group in espionage attacks against targets in Ukraine. The tooling supports stealthy persistence and data theft consistent with Turla's long-running intelligence-gathering operations.

stories.headline: Microsoft quietly extends free Windows 10 ESU support to October 2027
stories.summary: Microsoft quietly extended free Extended Security Updates for Windows 10 through October 2027, giving holdouts more time to receive critical patches before fully migrating. Defenders should still plan upgrades, but the extension reduces near-term exposure for unpatched fleets.

stories.headline: New 'Gaslight' macOS malware uses prompt injection to disrupt AI-assisted analysis
stories.summary: A new macOS malware strain, Gaslight, embeds fake errors and prompt-injection text designed to mislead AI-assisted analysis tools and slow incident response. It is an early example of malware authors targeting the AI tooling that defenders increasingly rely on.

stories.headline: Chrome ad blocker with 10M+ installs found carrying dormant script-injection code
stories.summary: A popular Chrome ad-blocking extension with more than 10 million installs was found to contain dormant script-injection capability that could be activated to run code in users' browsers. The case highlights the risk of trusted, high-install browser extensions being weaponised.

stories.headline: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN zero-day CVE-2026-20245 exploited to gain root access
stories.summary: Attackers exploited a Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager zero-day (CVE-2026-20245, CVSS 7.8) to gain root access, with the flaw listed in CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalogue. Organisations running affected SD-WAN Manager deployments should apply Cisco's updates without delay.

stories.headline: CISA warns critical Lantronix EDS5000 flaw is being actively exploited
stories.summary: CISA warned that a critical code-injection flaw in Lantronix EDS5000 device servers (CVE-2025-67038, CVSS 9.8) is being actively exploited and added it to the KEV catalogue. The bug affects industrial device-networking gear, raising risk for operational-technology environments.

stories.headline: Amadey and StealC malware network disrupted; 27 million stolen credentials recovered
stories.summary: A coordinated operation disrupted the infrastructure behind the Amadey and StealC infostealer malware families, recovering some 27 million stolen credentials. The takedown dents two widely used commodity stealers that feed credential-theft and follow-on intrusions.
